discussion_comments.comment_body:
Indeed, it's not required starting from RTL v9
https://github.com/testing-library/react-testing-library/releases/tag/v9.0.0
Still required, if test suite doesn't have afterEach set though
Is there any way to do this without snapshots? I'm not opposed to them if they are indeed the best choice, but when possible, I try to avoid them.
This is called automatically by RTL?

+  it('should compute canFetchMore correctly using initialData', async () => {
+    function Page() {
+      const fetchCountRef = React.useRef(0)
+      const {
+        status,
+        data,
+        error,
+        isFetching,
+        isFetchingMore,
+        fetchMore,
+        canFetchMore,
+        refetch,
+      } = useInfiniteQuery(
+        'items',
+        (key, nextId = 0) => fetchItems(nextId, fetchCountRef.current++),
+        {
+          initialData: [initialItems(0), initialItems(1)],
+          getFetchMore: (lastGroup, allGroups) => lastGroup.nextId,
+        }
+      )
+
+      expect(canFetchMore).not.toBeUndefined()

discussion_comments.comment_body:
I don't think you can use expect inside a component.
I would rather render `canFetchMore` inside a dom element and check it after component is rendered.
(In this case `canFetchMore` should be stringified, so "undefined" can be rendered as string)
